label: Sourcing and Enjoying Great Lakes Fish

comments.text: Listen: From fish that can survive in a stream just a few inches deep to fish that survive in the depths of Lake Michigan, “Fishes of the Chicago Region” serves to “expand your imagination," says outdoor columnist Dale Bowman. www.wbez.org/reset-with-s...
Fish and chips, Great Lakes style. Featuring world famous Little Bay de Noc lake perch caught earlier today. And it is ridiculously good. 🍽️ #mymichigan #906Life
They say you should eat more fish for your health..but maybe not THAT much? Commission for .bsky.social #transformation #furryart

See also: Great Lakes. Some of the best fish I ever ate came from the Great Lakes. When I bought it, it came with a warning that I didn’t notice until after I ate it- the recommended intake was one fish a YEAR. Cuz pollution was so bad in Great Lakes, it made fish little poison fillets.

It's beginning to look a lot like #Fishmas! 🐟🎄 That's right - #25DaysofFishmas will be on Bluesky this year! As in past years, I'll be highlighting a different #GreatLakes fish species each day in December, advent calendar-style. The 9th (!) year of #Fishmas kicks off December 1!

Fresh, still swimming as of this morning lake perch from Northwest Lake Michigan. There is nothing finer to eat in the lake than fresh Lake perch. And they are starting to come into shallower water making this a great time of year to fish for them. #906Life #PureMichigan

🎶Chestnuts roasting on an open fire, Jack Fish nipping at your nose 🎶 Day 3’s #Fishmas feature is a species that wouldn’t be out of place at the North Pole! The Northern Pike (Esox lucius), also known in Canada as jackfish, is a popular catch across the #GreatLakes and beyond #25DaysofFishmas

That’s no Ghost of #Fishmas Past! 👻 Day 19 features the Lake Sturgeon (Acipenser fulvescens), also known as by the Ojibwe names of Nmé/Nahmay, a prehistoric-looking #GreatLakes fish species whose story of resilience could rival any Dickens novel #25DaysofFishmas
For #GarWeek, here is a juvenile gar from southern Green Bay that we encountered this summer. Look that those pectoral fins go! After length measurement, this fish was released on site. #GreatLakes

Still so weird seeing a great egret stalking bait fish on the northwestern corner of Lake Michigan. Even though this guy's been coming by here three or four times a day now to feed his growing family. #906Life #PureMichigan #birds 🪿

Yippee-Ki-Yi, Master Fisher: no need to die hard this #Fishmas season because the Kiyi (Coregonus kiyi) is here to save the day - or at least slightly improve it! Part of a group of #GreatLakes whitefishes known as Ciscoes, the deepwater-dwelling Kiyi is the star of #25DaysofFishmas Day 4!

Prepare for trouble and make it double, because #Fishmas Day 8 brings one fish with two identities! Steelhead + Rainbow Trout are the same species - Oncorhynchus mykiss - with different lifestyles. While not native to the #GreatLakes, they're a favorite of anglers #SundayFishSketch #25DaysofFishmas

You don’t want to be spotted without the holiday season’s hottest accessory - spots! Dressed to impress with its namesake coloration, Day 18’s #Fishmas feature the Spotted Gar (Lepisosteus oculatus) might be the most stylish of the #GreatLakes gar species - no ugly holiday sweaters here! #GarWeek

Rudolph the Red-nose…Shiner? 🔴🦌 Red Shiner (Cyprinella lutrensis) - also called Red Horse Minnow or Rainbow Dace - leads us into #25DaysofFishmas Day 15 not with a nose so bright but with a cautionary tale of a potentially invasive species in the #GreatLakes (+ is the answer to #SundayFishSketch!)

🎶 I’ll have a blue #Fishmas without you...🎶 I’d definitely be blue celebrating #25DaysofFishmas without the Bluegill (Lepomis macrochirus), one of the #GreatLakes region’s most recognizable (and catchable 🎣) fishes!

And with that, Year 9 of #25DaysofFishmas is complete! Thank you to everyone who's followed along and shared these silly (and occasionally interesting) posts about #GreatLakes fish. I hope you have the chance to celebrate the amazing world just under the water's surface all year long!

Shortnose gar is one of 3 gar species found in the #GreatLakes region; the others being longnose + spotted gar. Shortnose - whose scientific name "platostomus" means "broad mouth" - is distinguished by a short (relative to the others) + broad snout (surprise!) as well as fewer spots #25DaysofFishmas
Decline of whitefish in Lake Michigan sparks fears that the fish might disappear The commercial harvest of the fish has declined nearly 90 percent since 1999 #GreatLakes www.wpr.org/news/decline...

Decades ago, non-native carp escaped into the Illinois River, wreaking havoc on the ecosystem. Now, a proposed project that’s both expensive and contentious aims to keep the fish out of the Great Lakes. 🐟

🎶I’m dreaming of a white #Fishmas…🎶 If you're dreaming of snow and cold this winter, you're in good company - the Pygmy Whitefish (Prosopium coulterii), is a small, little-known fish adapted for life in cold and dark environments like the deepest parts of Lake Superior #25DaysofFishmas ❄️

#Fishmas Day is here at last, so break out the candy canes because Day 25 is all about the stripes - the Striped Shiner (Luxilus chrysocephalus), that is! We end this year's #GreatLakes #25DaysofFishmas by shining some light on a small fish who’s not often in the spotlight ✨
Spring is here and the suckers are running. Here are the big two Great Lakes migratory sucker species, white and longnose. #sundayfishsketch

Why the long face? Even though #GarWeek is coming to a close, there’s still plenty of #Fishmas fun to be had! Finish hanging up the GAR-land on Day 22 of #25DaysofFishmas with the Longnose Gar (Lepisosteus osseus), one of the most distinctive and cool-looking fishes in the #GreatLakes

🎶From Atlantic to Pacific, gee the fish facts are terrific!🎶 #25DaysofFishmas is hitting the road! While there's no place like H.O.M.E.S. for the holidays, we'll be traveling beyond the #GreatLakes this year to learn about freshwater fishes from across the US. The road trip kicks off December 1!
A little late #SundayFishSketch with some fish observed last week. #GreatLakes

Last day of #Fishmas - perhaps the most beautiful sport fish on earth: the Arctic grayling. Iridescent rainbow colors with a huge sail-shaped dorsal fin. This fish is Santa's neighbor, living as far north as Northern Canada and Alaska, and south to the Great Lakes and Montana. Merry Christmas! 🐟

Need help wrapping your #Fishmas gifts? Maybe the BOWfin (Amia ocellicauda) can lend a fin! 🎁 Also known as the dogfish, this #GreatLakes native species is not as scary as it may look - one might even say that they’re all bark and no bite (unless you’re a small fish, of course) 🐶 #25DaysofFishmas

I was pregnant and advised not to eat fish from Michigan because of the high levels of mercury. Traveling in Ohio I asked a waitress where the fish was from. That I couldn’t have fish from the five lakes. She said that’s ok the fish is from Lake Erie …
Here in the Great Lakes Midwest you can tell your age to the month by how interested you are in next Friday’s fish fry
Fresh Little Bay de Noc lake perch. The bay is in the extreme Northwest corner of Lake Michigan. Pan-Fried and I'm keeping it simple with a homemade fried rice. 🍽️ #mymichigan

Of the 3 gar species found in the #GreatLakes, it's pretty easy to distinguish the longnose gar by its long, narrow snout that’s >2x as long as the rest of the head. While they do have some spots (more than the shortnose but fewer than the spotted), the schnoz really gives them away #25DaysofFishmas

Spring means warmer temperatures and the start of fish spawning. One of the earliest spawning fish in Wisconsin is the Northern Pike. Pike arrive right after ice out and use flooded areas. This gives their offspring a long first year growing season. #GreatLakes
A drawing of a ruffe, species uncertain. They are invasive in the Great Lakes and outcompetes several native fish. #SundayFIshSketch. #SciArt

An unfair bad rep: As an apex predator, bowfin help keep ecosystems balanced by preventing prey fish overpopulation. Despite looking similar to the invasive northern snakehead, bowfin are native to the #GreatLakes (and luckily, snakeheads haven’t been established in the lakes yet!) #25DaysofFishmas

🎶Sculpin’ around the #Fishmas tree🎶 On Day 10 of #25DaysofFishmas, we’re doing a deep dive with the Deepwater Sculpin (Myoxocephalus thompsonii), a small, bottom-dwelling #GreatLakes fish who’s not typically in the spotlight

From my unsuccessful comet hunting attempt a couple nights ago, I watched a Bald Eagle hunt fish for a bit before taking off to go roost for the evening. I’ve seen this species go from being extirpated locally when I was a kid to a regular breeder now. #birds #eagles #conservation #LakeErie

They stopped funding for "Stop the Carp" in our Great Lakes up here by Minnesota. It was designed to curb the invasive carp population from overtaking native fish and lake wildlife. We call these fish "suckers" as they are bottom feeders... Just like that 🍊🍕💩 causing all of this destruction!

Every year since time immeasurable… #SundayFishSketch teams up with #25DaysofFishmas (.bsky.social) for a beloved crossover! The #Fishytheme for Dec. 1 is to sketch a #GreatLakes fish described by the clues below - if you know the answer, don't spoil it, draw and post on Sunday! #sciart 🐡
For the Mimic theme of #sciartseptember and #SundayFishSketch, it's the mimic shiner! Probably because it looks like other shiners. Thanks shiners... #GreatLakes
For the boreal theme of #sciartseptember and #SundayFishSketch, a burbot! This freshwater cod species is found from the #GreatLakes and Midwest north to the boreal regions.
A #GarWeek #25DaysofFishmas #SundayFishSketch crossover with one of the coolest fish in the Great Lakes region, the longnose gar? It's a Christmas miracle!

Pickaliker - [ #fearsomecritters ] - a small subterranean, variant to the Ax-handle hound, known to nibble on pic-axes and other mining tools, leaving only the heads of axes and shovels behind. - Found local to the Duluth Minnesota & lake superior mines. ⛏️ Full story: samkalensky.com/products/pic...

I'm excited for this week's #SundayFishSketch & #25DaysofFishmas crossover! Earlier this year .bsky.social and I revised the genus and found five species of Pirate Perch! I painted the Western Pirate Perch, Aphredoderus gibbosus, from the #GreatLakes
Tiny Tim of Lake Superior, the pygmy whitefish. #25DaysOfFishmas
Lots of crinoids (?) on the beach. I believe this is a part of an ancient marine creature/invertebrate

WEEK 2 of the #SundayFishSketch team-up with #25DaysofFishmas (.bsky.social) for a difficult #Fishytheme. Sketch the #GreatLakes fish described by the clues below - if you know the answer, don't spoil it, draw and post on Sunday! #sciart 🐡🦑

White suckers spawning in Silver Creek, Manitowoc, WI. These fish migrate into tributaries from the #GreatLakes and we're here to greet them. This Creek is one of many that .bsky.social from Shedd Aquarium has volunteers looking for suckers in the spring!

A significant part of my illustration portfolio is freshwater fishes of the Great Lakes region. I created a collage of them for this puzzle design. The puzzle is made & sold by The Occurrence, a gem of a company in Ontario that ships worldwide: www.theoccurrence.ca/product/fish... #fishart #sciart

On Dasher, on Darter! It’s #Fishmas Eve, but who needs Rudolph’s nose when your entire body is brighter than holiday lights? 🦌 The penultimate fish of this year’s #GreatLakes #25DaysofFishmas is the Gilt Darter (Percina evides), a small, colorful fish that brings the holiday sparkles and gilt ✨

With the first day of #25DaysofFishmas falling on Sunday, it's time for another year of #SundayFishSketch + #25DaysofFishmas crossovers! The theme for December 1 is to sketch the #GreatLakes fish species described by these clues - if you know the answer, don't spoil it! .bsky.social

Kiyi is part of the Coregonus (whitefish) genus of fish which falls within the Salmon family. It's believed that there were actually two subspecies historically found in the #GreatLakes (Upper Great Lakes Kiyi + Lake Ontario Kiyi) but only the Upper Great Lakes kiyi remains today #25DaysofFishmas

The spotted gar looks similar to both of the other gar species found in the #GreatLakes (shortnose + longnose), but can be distinguished by its short(er) nose and presence of lots o’ spots all over the body + head - not surprising, given their scientific name “oculatus” means "eyed” #25DaysofFishmas
Blazingstar and a white sucker for #SundayFishSketch. The blazingstar was in our yard and the sucker was caught in Green Bay last week during wetland surveys. #GreatLakes
Lake Sturgeon for the (I couldn't find the theme) theme. Ancient fish swimming around the Great Lakes and Midwest. #sundayfishsketch
can be modified for use in the Upper #GreatLakes as "fish around and find eelpout" .bsky.social
When we did some beach seining ladt week in Lake Michigan the target was larval coregonines, but mostly we caught mimic shiners #SundayFishSketch
My daughter caught a fish on Lake Superior in Michigan last summer.
In the dark depths of Lake Superior, it's the Kiyi. #SundayFishSketch

There are 4 species of sculpins found in the #GreatLakes - deepwater, spoonhead, slimy + mottled - that (generally) occupy different depths. The mottled sculpin is typically found in shallow water, while the deepwater sculpin has been documented ~1300 ft /400 m deep in Lake Superior #25DaysofFishmas
please stay safe out there, fishes of lake michigan 😟😨

December's cold and dark - just perfect for the Kiyi! They live in the deepest, coldest waters of the #GreatLakes + rarely found <330 ft/100 m. In contrast to other ciscoes, their large eyes have evolved to see particularly well in the deep's low-light #25DaysofFishmas www.ijc.org/en/kiyi-eyes...

Travel from the comfort of your own home this #25DaysofFishmas as we explore fishes from all across the US each day in December. Best part? No sleeping on air mattresses in guest rooms! The road trip kicks off with fishes from the southeastern US on Monday Dec. 1!

For the #SundayFishSketch of fish I'd like to catch, I went with this larval fish I caught last week. Something from the whitefish family but who knows what. I'd like to eat some this year! #GreatLakes
Okay, yes, I am now back eating fish and such. Nice lunch on the Grand River. Crab cakes, then Lake Perch.
For the "hidden" theme of #sundayfishsketch and #sciartseptember is the kiyi. A species that hides deep in the dark of Lake Superior during the day and moves toward the surface at night.

When the gars align... ⭐⭐ That's right, #25DaysofFishmas will be coinciding with #GarWeek this year! Stay tuned next week for some special #Fishmas features about #GreatLakes Lepisosteidae (& Friends) .bsky.social .bsky.social

Continuing our most festive of crossovers, the #SundayFishSketch #Fishytheme clues for Sunday are below! Take (and then draw) a guess at what is #25DaysofFishmas (.bsky.social) Great Lakes fish is and post your drawings this Sunday! #sciart 🦑🐡

There are 13 recognized species in the bluegill’s genus (Lepomis) including others common in the #GreatLakes like pumpkinseed + green sunfish. To make things extra interesting, these species can often hybridize with each other leading to some aptly-named hybrids like the pumpkingill #25DaysofFishmas

Despite similarities (small, brown-ish, hang out on the bottom), the deepwater sculpin is a native #GreatLakes species unlike the invasive round goby. How to tell them apart? Flip ‘em over! The goby's pelvic (belly) fins are fused together into a disc but the sculpin's are separate #25DaysofFishmas

One way to get silver carp, a threat to Great Lakes fisheries, under control seems relatively simple – eat them. The white, flaky flesh of the fish is rich in nutrients and a staple food in Chinese cuisine, but it’s less popular with Americans. Via Louisiana Illuminator

It's time for Week 2 of the #SundayFishSketch - #25DaysofFishmas mystery species theme! Using the clues below, sketch the #GreatLakes fish species that you think is being described and post your art this Sunday (December 8) - no spoiling if you know what it is! .bsky.social

June 30th, 1991. I caught a big walleye from Lake Erie. Actually, if I remember correctly, someone else on the boat hooked the fish, then I reeled it in. Still counts, I think 😄🎣
Silver carp are an invasive species that don't have stomachs so eat CONSTANTLY. They starve out other fish as a result. They are heading towards the Great Lakes.

When invasive round goby came to the #GreatLakes, smallmouth bass took quite a liking to them, with gobies becoming the dominant prey of smallmouths in Lake Erie. In fact, round goby is consumed more by smallmouth bass than by any other Lake Erie fish species #25DaysofFishmas doi.org/10.1016/j.jg...

Deepwater sculpins are found in northern North America, almost always in deep lakes (where they often hang out at depths of >100 ft/35 m) with temps colder than 45°F/8°C. Their range extends from Quebec + the #GreatLakes in the east to the Northwest Territories in the west #25DaysofFishmas

#FishInTheNews: Great Lakes fish thought extinct for decades rediscovered – in an unexpected place A native Great Lakes whitefish thought extinct for nearly 40 years has been rediscovered by scientists – in the wrong Great Lake. Story: www.usatoday.com/story/news/n...

The Kiyi is the Tiny Tim of the group of whitefishes called “ciscoes,” a term used to collectively refer to the 8-ish species of the Coregonus genus that are or were once found in the #GreatLakes. Kiyi often only grow to ~9 in / 25 cm long #25DaysofFishmas
It's sucker season and here's a longnose sucker that might be heading upstream to spawn. #sundayfishsketch #GreatLakes

The black bullhead is native to central North America from the Hudson Bay and #GreatLakes basins down to northern Mexico. They’re pretty hardy bottom-dwelling fish who can tolerate a wide range of conditions including waters with low oxygen, high murkiness, and hot temperatures #25DaysofFishmas
